items.title: Representation, equality and container protocols: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Returning False from __eq__ for every unknown type is always correct and defining __eq__ leaves inherited hashing valid automatically.

feedback.correct: Correct. Honor each data-model protocol: return NotImplemented for unsupported equality, align equality with hashing, and keep representation and iteration predictable.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Honor each data-model protocol: return NotImplemented for unsupported equality, align equality with hashing, and keep representation and iteration predictable.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.
